articles.headline: India Unveils Ambitious Green Hydrogen Export Strategy, Eyes Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) launched the 'National Green Hydrogen Mission - Phase II', focusing on scaling up production for domestic use and establishing India as a global export hub.
• The strategy includes incentives for green ammonia production and infrastructure development for port-based export facilities.
• Key targets include reducing the cost of green hydrogen production and developing indigenous electrolyser manufacturing capabilities.
• Emphasis is placed on international collaborations for technology transfer and market access.
• This phase aims to create over 500,000 jobs and significantly reduce carbon emissions in hard-to-abate sectors.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Energy
Infrastructure
Investment models
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched by MNRE.
Phase II focuses on export hub status and green ammonia.
Incentives for indigenous electrolyser manufacturing.
Targets for job creation and emission reduction.
Green hydrogen production involves electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's potential to become a global leader in green hydrogen production and export, highlighting the economic and environmental benefits.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with scaling up green hydrogen production and developing export infrastructure.
Evaluate the role of government policies and international collaborations in achieving the goals of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Examine the impact of green hydrogen adoption on India's energy security and its commitment to climate action.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background, signifying renewable energy sources.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Renewable Energy and Climate Change Initiatives

articles.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Federal Fiscal Powers, Emphasizes Cooperative Federalism
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment on a batch of petitions concerning the fiscal autonomy and taxation powers of states vis-à-vis the Union government.
• The ruling reiterated the importance of cooperative federalism in financial matters, emphasizing consultation and consensus-building.
• It clarified the scope of 'residuary powers' of taxation and the mechanism for dispute resolution between the Centre and states over revenue sharing.
• The judgment is expected to impact future inter-state financial relations and the implementation of GST compensation mechanisms.
• Legal experts believe the verdict strengthens the financial independence of states within the constitutional framework.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court ruling on Centre-state fiscal relations.
Emphasizes cooperative federalism and financial autonomy of states.
Clarifies 'residuary powers' of taxation.
Impacts GST compensation mechanisms and inter-state financial disputes.
Article 246 of the Constitution deals with the subject matter of laws made by Parliament and State Legislatures.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the financial relations between the Union and states, particularly in the context of GST.
Discuss how the concept of cooperative federalism is reinforced by this ruling and its potential to resolve fiscal disputes.
Examine the constitutional provisions governing taxation and financial devolution in India, and how this judgment interprets them.
Critically evaluate the challenges faced by states in achieving fiscal autonomy and the role of judicial interventions in balancing powers.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The Indian Constitution lists 15 taxes that can be levied by the Union and 20 by the states.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Stages, Focus on Critical Minerals and Tech
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) are reportedly in their concluding phase, with key discussions revolving around market access for critical minerals and technology transfer.
• Both sides aim to finalize the agreement by the end of 2025, potentially unlocking significant trade and investment opportunities.
• India is pushing for greater access to European markets for its services sector and agricultural products.
• The EU seeks reduction in tariffs on industrial goods and stronger intellectual property rights protections.
• The FTA is expected to boost bilateral trade by an estimated 40-50% over the next five years.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India-EU FTA negotiations nearing completion.
Key areas: critical minerals, technology transfer, services, IPR.
Aims to increase bilateral trade significantly.
EU is India's third-largest trading partner.
FTAs reduce tariffs and non-tariff barriers between member countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic implications of a potential India-EU FTA for India's trade, investment, and geopolitical standing.
Discuss the key contentious issues in the India-EU FTA negotiations and how they are being addressed.
Evaluate the benefits and challenges for India's domestic industries from increased competition under the FTA.
Examine how the FTA could contribute to diversifying India's supply chains and enhancing its technological capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: The EU is the world's largest trading bloc and India's second-largest trading partner after the US.

articles.headline: New National E-Waste Management Policy 2025 Emphasizes Circular Economy and Extended Producer Responsibility
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) unveiled the 'National E-Waste Management Policy 2025', aiming for a comprehensive and sustainable approach to electronic waste.
• The policy strengthens the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework, making manufacturers more accountable for the entire lifecycle of their products.
• It promotes the formalization of the e-waste recycling sector and encourages the development of indigenous recycling technologies.
• Key provisions include targets for collection and recycling, awareness campaigns, and penalties for non-compliance.
• The policy aligns with India's commitment to circular economy principles and resource efficiency.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: National E-Waste Management Policy 2025 launched by MoEFCC.
Strengthens Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework.
Promotes formalization of recycling sector and indigenous tech.
Includes collection targets and penalties for non-compliance.
India is the third-largest producer of e-waste globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical challenges posed by e-waste in India and how the new policy aims to address them through a circular economy approach.
Analyze the effectiveness of the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework in promoting sustainable e-waste management.
Evaluate the role of technology and public awareness in successful implementation of the E-Waste Management Policy 2025.
Examine the socio-economic impact of formalizing the e-waste recycling sector and its implications for informal workers.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic gadgets symbolizing e-waste, with a green recycling symbol superimposed.
articles.trivia: Only about 17.4% of global e-waste was formally collected and recycled in 2019.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Deploys Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Disaster Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched and deployed a new generation advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• EOS-07 is equipped with high-resolution optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payloads, providing all-weather, all-time imaging capabilities.
• The satellite will significantly enhance India's capabilities in disaster management, agricultural monitoring, urban planning, and environmental surveillance.
• This mission reinforces India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in space technology and its commitment to providing crucial data for national development.
• Data from EOS-07 will be critical for predicting and mitigating the impacts of natural calamities like floods, cyclones, and droughts.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched EOS-07 from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
EOS-07 features high-resolution optical and SAR payloads.
Enhances disaster management, agriculture, urban planning.
Supports 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in space technology.
SAR can penetrate clouds and darkness, providing all-weather data.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of advanced Earth Observation Satellites like EOS-07 for India's national development and disaster preparedness.
Analyze how ISRO's indigenous capabilities in space technology contribute to India's self-reliance and global standing.
Examine the various applications of remote sensing data in sectors such as agriculture, urban planning, and environmental monitoring.
Evaluate the challenges and future prospects for India's space program in developing next-generation earth observation systems.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustration of the EOS-07 satellite orbiting Earth, with various data streams depicted.
articles.trivia: ISRO operates one of the largest constellations of remote sensing satellites in the world.

articles.headline: RBI Governor Reiterates Commitment to Inflation Targeting Amidst Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor Shaktikanta Das, in a recent address, reaffirmed the central bank's unwavering commitment to achieving and maintaining price stability.
• He noted that while global economic uncertainties persist, including geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility, India's domestic economic fundamentals remain robust.
• The Governor emphasized the need for a calibrated monetary policy approach to balance growth imperatives with inflation management.
• The RBI is closely monitoring food inflation, which has been a key driver of headline inflation in recent months.
• Analysts anticipate the RBI to maintain a cautious stance on interest rates, awaiting clearer signals on sustained inflation moderation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: RBI Governor reiterated focus on inflation targeting.
Global uncertainties cited as challenges.
Monetary policy aims to balance growth and inflation.
Food inflation remains a concern.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the policy interest rate to achieve the inflation target.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Analyze the tools and strategies employed by the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) to achieve its inflation targets.
Evaluate the impact of global geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility on India's macroeconomic stability.
Examine the interlinkages between food inflation and overall headline inflation in India and the policy responses required.
articles.imageDescription: The RBI logo prominently displayed on a building, symbolizing economic stability.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Formed for Extensive Consultations on Uniform Civil Code (UCC)
articles.explainer:
• Amidst intensified discussions on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC), a high-level parliamentary committee has been constituted to engage with various stakeholders.
• The committee comprises legal experts, community leaders, and representatives from different political parties, aiming for broad-based consultations.
• Its mandate includes studying existing personal laws, analyzing their implications, and gathering public opinion on a potential UCC.
• The move signals the government's intent to build consensus before introducing any legislative framework, addressing concerns about religious and cultural sensitivities.
• The debate surrounding UCC touches upon fundamental rights, secularism, and gender justice.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary committee formed for UCC consultations.
Mandate includes studying personal laws and gathering public opinion.
UCC mentioned in Article 44 of the Indian Constitution (DPSP).
Aims to build consensus on a sensitive issue.
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate and historical background of the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India.
Analyze the arguments for and against the implementation of a UCC, considering aspects of religious freedom, gender justice, and national integration.
Evaluate the challenges involved in drafting and implementing a UCC in a diverse country like India, and how a parliamentary committee can facilitate consensus.
Examine the potential impact of a UCC on personal laws and the concept of secularism in India.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and an open book of law, symbolizing legal deliberation.
articles.trivia: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution states that 'The State shall endeavour to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India.'

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Directive Principles of State Policy and Fundamental Rights

articles.headline: India Hosts 'Global South Summit' Advocating for Equitable Climate Finance and Technology Access
articles.explainer:
• India is hosting the 'Global South Summit 2025', bringing together leaders from developing nations to address pressing issues of climate finance, technology transfer, and sustainable development.
• The summit aims to build a common agenda and amplify the voices of the Global South on international platforms like the G20 and COP meetings.
• Discussions focused on reforming multilateral development banks to ensure greater access to affordable climate finance for developing countries.
• India emphasized the need for equitable and inclusive technology access, particularly for renewable energy and climate resilience solutions.
• The Delhi Declaration, adopted at the summit, calls for enhanced south-south cooperation and collective action on global challenges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India hosts Global South Summit 2025.
Focus on climate finance, technology access, sustainable development.
Aims to amplify Global South's voice in G20, COP.
Calls for reform of multilateral development banks.
Delhi Declaration adopted for south-south cooperation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's leadership in advocating for the interests of the Global South on issues like climate finance and technology transfer.
Analyze the challenges faced by developing countries in securing adequate climate finance and access to green technologies.
Evaluate the role of South-South cooperation in addressing global challenges and promoting sustainable development.
Examine how the outcomes of the Global South Summit could influence discussions at major international forums like G20 and COP.
articles.imageDescription: A world map highlighting the countries of the Global South, with India's location prominent.
articles.trivia: The term 'Global South' generally refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America, often characterized by developing economies and a history of colonialism.

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'National Cyber Security Strategy 2025' to Fortify Digital Defenses
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released the 'National Cyber Security Strategy 2025' to address the escalating threats in the digital landscape.
• The strategy focuses on a multi-pronged approach, including strengthening critical information infrastructure, promoting cyber hygiene, and fostering indigenous cyber security capabilities.
• Key pillars include robust threat intelligence sharing mechanisms, enhancing cyber forensics, and developing a skilled cybersecurity workforce.
• It also emphasizes international cooperation to combat trans-border cyber crimes and ensure a secure global digital commons.
• The policy aims to create a resilient and trusted cyber ecosystem for citizens and businesses, supporting India's digital economy aspirations.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Cyber security
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: National Cyber Security Strategy 2025 released by MeitY.
Focus on critical infrastructure, cyber hygiene, indigenous capabilities.
Pillars: threat intelligence, forensics, skilled workforce.
Emphasizes international cooperation against cyber crime.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for cyber security incidents.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats in India and how the 'National Cyber Security Strategy 2025' addresses these challenges.
Analyze the importance of strengthening critical information infrastructure and promoting cyber hygiene for national security and economic stability.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and indigenous capability building in creating a resilient cyber ecosystem.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive cyber security strategy, including workforce development and public awareness.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon overlaid on a map of India, symbolizing cybersecurity for the nation.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top 10 countries most targeted by cyberattacks globally.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Major Milestone, Integrates Millions of Health Records
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced a significant milestone, successfully integrating over 500 million health records into its digital ecosystem.
• This achievement marks a crucial step towards creating a seamless and interoperable digital health infrastructure across the country.
• ABDM aims to provide every citizen with a unique Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) number, linking their health records digitally.
• The mission facilitates easier access to healthcare services, promotes telemedicine, and enables efficient data-driven policy-making.
• This progress is vital for improving healthcare delivery, especially in remote and underserved areas, and ensures continuity of care.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: ABDM integrated over 500 million health records.
Aims to create a seamless digital health infrastructure.
Provides unique Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) number.
Facilitates telemedicine and data-driven policy-making.
ABDM was launched by the Prime Minister in 2021.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in revolutionizing healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of integrating health records digitally, particularly concerning data privacy and security.
Evaluate how ABDM can improve access to healthcare services, especially for vulnerable populations and in remote areas.
Examine the role of digital health initiatives in achieving universal health coverage and strengthening public health infrastructure.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing a network of interconnected health icons, representing digital health records.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government Health Schemes and Digital Initiatives

articles.headline: Infrastructure Spending Boost Announced for Rural Connectivity Under PMGSY
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Rural Development announced a significant increase in infrastructure spending for rural road connectivity under the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY).
• The additional allocation aims to accelerate the completion of remaining unconnected habitations and upgrade existing rural road networks.
• Emphasis is placed on using green technologies and sustainable materials for road construction, reducing environmental impact.
• The initiative is expected to boost economic activity in rural areas, improve access to markets, healthcare, and education, and create local employment.
• PMGSY has been a flagship program for rural development, connecting millions of villages since its inception.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Increased spending for PMGSY announced by Ministry of Rural Development.
Aims to connect remaining habitations and upgrade roads.
Focus on green technologies and sustainable materials.
Expected to boost rural economy and employment.
PMGSY was launched in 2000.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of rural road connectivity in fostering inclusive economic growth and social development in India.
Analyze the impact of increased infrastructure spending under PMGSY on rural livelihoods, access to services, and regional disparities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of using green technologies and sustainable materials in rural road construction.
Examine the challenges in achieving universal rural road connectivity and the policy measures required for sustainable maintenance of rural infrastructure.
articles.imageDescription: A well-constructed rural road winding through green fields, symbolizing improved connectivity.
articles.trivia: PMGSY aims to provide all-weather road connectivity to eligible unconnected habitations in rural areas.

articles.headline: Tiger Census 2025 Shows Stable Growth, Highlights Concerns Over Habitat Fragmentation
articles.explainer:
• The latest Tiger Census (2025) released by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) indicates a stable and healthy growth in India's tiger population.
• While the overall numbers are encouraging, the report highlights increasing concerns regarding habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict outside protected areas.
• The census employed advanced camera trapping and DNA analysis techniques for accurate estimation.
• Recommendations include establishing more wildlife corridors, engaging local communities in conservation efforts, and managing buffer zones effectively.
• India is home to approximately 70% of the world's wild tiger population, making its conservation efforts globally significant.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Tiger Census 2025 released by NTCA.
Shows stable growth in tiger population.
Highlights concerns: habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict.
Recommendations: wildlife corridors, community engagement.
India hosts 70% of global wild tiger population. Project Tiger launched in 1973.
mainsPoints: Discuss the successes and challenges of tiger conservation efforts in India, with a focus on habitat protection and management.
Analyze the causes and consequences of habitat fragmentation and human-wildlife conflict on tiger populations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various conservation strategies, including wildlife corridors and community participation, in mitigating threats to tigers.
Examine India's role as a leader in global tiger conservation and the lessons that can be drawn from its experience.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger captured in its natural habitat, symbolizing successful conservation.
articles.trivia: India conducts a tiger census every four years.

articles.headline: India Showcases Indigenous 5G Advanced Chipsets, Bolstering 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in Telecom
articles.explainer:
• Indian researchers and companies have successfully developed and showcased indigenous 5G Advanced chipsets, marking a significant leap towards self-reliance in telecommunications technology.
• These chipsets are designed for advanced 5G use cases, including ultra-low latency applications, massive IoT connectivity, and enhanced mobile broadband.
• The development is a result of collaborative efforts between academia, startups, and government research institutions under the 'Digital India' and 'Make in India' initiatives.
• This achievement is expected to reduce India's dependence on foreign technology and boost its position in the global semiconductor value chain.
• It paves the way for secure and customized 5G network deployments, critical for national security and economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Indigenous 5G Advanced chipsets developed in India.
Designed for ultra-low latency, massive IoT, enhanced mobile broadband.
Result of collaboration under 'Digital India' and 'Make in India'.
Reduces foreign technology dependence, boosts semiconductor position.
5G Advanced (5G-A) is the next evolution of 5G technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of developing indigenous 5G Advanced chipsets for India's telecommunications sector and its 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision.
Analyze the economic and strategic advantages of reducing reliance on foreign technology in critical sectors like telecommunications.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives, academia, and industry collaboration in fostering innovation and indigenous technology development.
Examine the potential impact of indigenous 5G technology on India's digital transformation, national security, and global competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: A microchip with 5G connectivity symbols, representing advanced telecom technology.
articles.trivia: India's first 5G test bed was inaugurated in May 2022.

articles.headline: State Election Commission Announces Dates for Key Assembly Elections, Model Code of Conduct in Effect
articles.explainer:
• The State Election Commission (SEC) announced the schedule for upcoming assembly elections in three crucial states, triggering the immediate enforcement of the Model Code of Conduct (MCC).
• The elections are expected to be keenly contested, with major national and regional parties vying for power.
• The MCC imposes restrictions on political parties and candidates to ensure a level playing field and prevent malpractices during the election period.
• Security arrangements are being scaled up, and electoral rolls have been updated to ensure fair and transparent polls.
• The outcome of these elections will be closely watched for their implications on national politics and regional power dynamics.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
prelimsPoints: State Election Commission announced assembly election dates.
Model Code of Conduct (MCC) immediately enforced.
MCC aims for fair elections and prevents malpractices.
Electoral rolls updated for transparent polls.
The Election Commission of India conducts elections for Parliament, State Legislatures, President, and Vice President. State Election Commissions conduct local body elections.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) in ensuring free and fair elections in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Election Commission in enforcing the MCC and managing electoral malpractices.
Evaluate the impact of state assembly election outcomes on national political narratives and federal dynamics.
Examine the electoral reforms needed to further strengthen the integrity and transparency of the Indian electoral process.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with a hand casting a vote, symbolizing the democratic process.
articles.trivia: The Model Code of Conduct is a set of guidelines issued by the Election Commission of India for the conduct of political parties and candidates during elections.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for UN Security Council Reform at High-Level Dialogue
articles.explainer:
• India once again underscored the urgent need for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) at a high-level dialogue on multilateralism.
• India emphasized that the current structure of the UNSC does not reflect the contemporary geopolitical realities and is outdated.
• The call for reform includes expansion of both permanent and non-permanent categories of membership to ensure greater representation for developing countries.
• India highlighted its strong credentials for a permanent seat, citing its demographic size, economic power, democratic values, and contributions to peacekeeping.
• The dialogue focused on strengthening multilateral institutions to address global challenges more effectively and inclusively.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India calls for UNSC reform at high-level dialogue.
Current UNSC structure does not reflect contemporary realities.
Seeks expansion of both permanent and non-permanent seats.
India cites its credentials for a permanent seat.
UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the reform of the UN Security Council, with specific reference to India's position and aspirations.
Analyze how the existing structure of the UNSC impacts its effectiveness in addressing contemporary global challenges.
Evaluate India's contributions to global peace and security that bolster its claim for a permanent seat in the UNSC.
Examine the challenges and prospects of achieving meaningful reforms in multilateral institutions like the United Nations.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations headquarters building in New York, with the UN flag.
articles.trivia: The five permanent members of the UNSC are China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States.

articles.headline: New National Scheme Launched to Skill Youth in Emerging Technologies for Future Workforce
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched a new national scheme, 'Yuva Kaushal 4.0', aimed at skilling and upskilling India's youth in emerging technologies.
• The scheme focuses on areas such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), Data Science, Blockchain, Cybersecurity, and Robotics.
• It involves collaboration with industry leaders and academic institutions to design job-oriented courses and provide practical training.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the skill gap in the rapidly evolving digital economy and enhance the employability of the Indian workforce.
• 'Yuva Kaushal 4.0' is a crucial step towards leveraging India's demographic dividend and preparing for the demands of Industry 4.0.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: New scheme 'Yuva Kaushal 4.0' launched by Ministry of Skill Development.
Focuses on skilling youth in AI, ML, Data Science, Blockchain, Cybersecurity, Robotics.
Aims to bridge skill gap in digital economy.
Prepares workforce for Industry 4.0.
National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) is a key implementing agency for skill development initiatives.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of skilling India's youth in emerging technologies to harness its demographic dividend and meet the demands of Industry 4.0.
Analyze the challenges in bridging the skill gap in a rapidly evolving technological landscape and how 'Yuva Kaushal 4.0' addresses these.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and industry collaboration in designing and delivering effective skill development programs.
Examine the potential impact of such schemes on youth employment, economic growth, and India's competitiveness in the global digital economy.
articles.imageDescription: A group of diverse young people engaged in learning on digital devices, with futuristic tech symbols.
articles.trivia: India has the world's largest youth population, with over 66% of its total population under the age of 35.

articles.headline: India Joins International Call for Phased Reform of Global Fossil Fuel Subsidies
articles.explainer:
• India, alongside several other nations, participated in a high-level international forum advocating for a phased reform of global fossil fuel subsidies.
• The discussions highlighted the economic inefficiencies and environmental harm caused by such subsidies, urging a transition towards cleaner energy sources.
• India emphasized a 'just transition' approach, ensuring that reforms do not disproportionately impact vulnerable populations or developing economies.
• The forum explored mechanisms for international cooperation to facilitate subsidy reforms, including sharing best practices and financial support.
• This move aligns with India's climate commitments and its efforts to promote renewable energy, while also navigating energy security concerns.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Energy
prelimsPoints: India advocates for phased reform of global fossil fuel subsidies.
Highlights economic inefficiencies and environmental harm.
Emphasizes 'just transition' approach for vulnerable populations.
Seeks international cooperation for reform mechanisms.
Fossil fuel subsidies can distort markets and hinder renewable energy adoption.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic and environmental rationale behind reforming fossil fuel subsidies globally and in India.
Analyze the concept of 'just transition' in the context of fossil fuel subsidy reforms and its importance for developing countries.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in phasing out fossil fuel subsidies while ensuring energy security and affordability.
Examine how international cooperation can facilitate a smoother and more equitable global transition away from fossil fuels.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing a shift from fossil fuel power plants to wind turbines and solar panels, symbolizing energy transition.
articles.trivia: The IMF estimates that global fossil fuel subsidies amounted to $5.9 trillion in 2020.

articles.headline: India Enhances Engagement in Arctic Governance, Focus on Climate Research and Resource Management
articles.explainer:
• India announced enhanced engagement in Arctic governance, reiterating its commitment to scientific research, environmental protection, and sustainable resource management in the region.
• This follows India's updated Arctic Policy, which emphasizes strengthening scientific collaboration with Arctic nations and understanding the impact of Arctic warming on the Indian monsoon.
• India plans to expand its research station 'Himadri' and deploy more scientific expeditions to study climate change, glaciology, and biodiversity.
• The focus also includes exploring potential for sustainable resource extraction, adhering to international norms and environmental safeguards.
• India's increasing presence in the Arctic underscores its growing strategic interests in global commons and climate diplomacy.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India enhances engagement in Arctic governance.
Focus on climate research, environmental protection, resource management.
Updated Arctic Policy emphasizes scientific collaboration.
Plans to expand 'Himadri' research station.
India is an observer state in the Arctic Council.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and scientific importance of the Arctic region for India and the rationale behind its enhanced engagement in Arctic governance.
Analyze the potential impacts of Arctic warming on India's climate, particularly the monsoon, and the role of Indian research in this area.
Evaluate the opportunities and challenges for India in sustainable resource management and environmental protection in the Arctic.
Examine how India's Arctic policy contributes to its broader climate diplomacy and its role as a responsible global actor.
articles.imageDescription: A map of the Arctic region with India's research station Himadri marked, and a compass rose.
articles.trivia: India established its first Arctic research station, Himadri, in Ny-Ålesund, Svalbard, Norway, in 2008.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: India's Foreign Policy and Polar Regions

articles.headline: New Research Sheds Light on Potential Approaches to Deciphering Ancient Indus Valley Script
articles.explainer:
• A team of international researchers has published new findings proposing novel computational and linguistic approaches to decipher the enigmatic Indus Valley Script.
• The research utilizes advanced AI and pattern recognition techniques to analyze the approximately 4,000 surviving inscriptions from the Harappan civilization.
• While a definitive decipherment remains elusive, the study suggests potential linguistic structures and phonetic patterns within the script, hinting at its underlying language family.
• The Indus Valley Script, one of the world's oldest writing systems, has baffled archaeologists and linguists for decades due to the absence of bilingual inscriptions.
• Successful decipherment could unlock unprecedented insights into the social, economic, and religious life of the ancient Indus Valley Civilization.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: New research on Indus Valley Script decipherment using AI.
Indus Script has ~4,000 inscriptions, no bilingual keys.
Potential linguistic structures and phonetic patterns identified.
Indus Valley Civilization sites like Harappa, Mohenjo-Daro.
The script is primarily pictographic and logo-syllabic.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Valley Script and the challenges faced by researchers in its decipherment.
Analyze how modern computational and linguistic approaches are offering new avenues for understanding ancient writing systems.
Evaluate the potential impact of a successful decipherment of the Indus Script on our understanding of ancient Indian history and culture.
Examine the key features of the Indus Valley Civilization that could be further illuminated by insights from its script.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up image of a Harappan seal bearing inscriptions of the Indus Valley Script.
articles.trivia: The longest Indus script inscription has only 26 symbols, indicating that it might not be a purely alphabetical script.

highlights: India launches 'National Green Hydrogen Mission - Phase II' to become a global export hub for green energy.
Supreme Court clarifies federal fiscal powers, emphasizing cooperative federalism in Centre-state financial relations.
India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations enter final stages, focusing on critical minerals and technology transfer.
ISRO successfully deploys advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07) for enhanced disaster monitoring and national development.
Government unveils 'National Cyber Security Strategy 2025' to fortify digital defenses and promote indigenous capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National Green Hydrogen Mission', consider the following statements:
1. It is launched by the Ministry of Power.
2. Phase II primarily focuses on establishing India as a global export hub for green hydrogen and green ammonia.
3. The mission aims to develop indigenous electrolyser manufacturing capabilities.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The National Green Hydrogen Mission is launched by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE). Statement 2 is correct: Phase II of the mission emphasizes scaling up production for domestic use and establishing India as a global export hub for green hydrogen and green ammonia. Statement 3 is correct: A key component of the mission is to provide incentives for indigenous electrolyser manufacturing to reduce import dependence.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution deals with the subject matter of laws made by Parliament and State Legislatures, and also forms the basis for Centre-State fiscal relations?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Article 246 of the Indian Constitution distributes legislative powers between the Union and State Legislatures by enumerating subjects in the Union List, State List, and Concurrent List. This distribution of legislative powers directly impacts Centre-State fiscal relations as taxation powers are derived from these lists. Article 280 pertains to the Finance Commission, Article 268 to duties levied by the Union but collected and appropriated by the States, and Article 301 deals with freedom of trade, commerce, and intercourse.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM):
1. It aims to provide every citizen with a unique Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) number.
2. The mission focuses exclusively on providing health insurance coverage to the poor and vulnerable.
3. It facilitates easier access to healthcare services and promotes telemedicine.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: ABDM aims to provide every citizen with a unique Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) number to link their health records digitally. Statement 2 is incorrect: While related, ABDM focuses on creating a digital health infrastructure and managing health records, not exclusively on providing health insurance coverage. The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY) is the health insurance component. Statement 3 is correct: The mission facilitates easier access to healthcare services, promotes telemedicine, and enables efficient data-driven policy-making.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a key feature of the 'National Cyber Security Strategy 2025'?
options: (b) Promoting cyber hygiene among citizens and businesses.
(c) Developing indigenous cyber security capabilities and workforce.
(d) Establishing a global cyber army for offensive operations.
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) Establishing a global cyber army for offensive operations.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'National Cyber Security Strategy 2025' focuses on defensive measures, resilience, and international cooperation to combat cyber threats. Options (a), (b), and (c) are all key features of the strategy, aimed at creating a robust and trusted cyber ecosystem. Option (d) describes an offensive military capability, which is not stated as a public policy objective of the strategy.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Indus Valley Civilization, consider the following statements:
1. The Indus Script has been definitively deciphered using bilingual inscriptions.
2. Recent research employs AI and pattern recognition techniques to analyze the script.
3. Harappa and Mohenjo-Daro are prominent sites associated with this civilization.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Indus Script remains largely undeciphered, primarily due to the absence of bilingual inscriptions (like the Rosetta Stone for Egyptian hieroglyphs). Statement 2 is correct: New research is indeed utilizing advanced AI and pattern recognition techniques to analyze the script and identify potential linguistic structures. Statement 3 is correct: Harappa and Mohenjo-Daro are two of the largest and most well-known urban centers of the ancient Indus Valley Civilization.

mainsMocks.question: The National Green Hydrogen Mission's Phase II aims to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen exports. Discuss the strategic implications of this ambition for India's energy security, climate commitments, and economic growth. What challenges might India face in achieving this goal?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its export focus. Discuss strategic implications: reduced fossil fuel imports (energy security), meeting NDC targets (climate), job creation and economic diversification (economic growth). Outline challenges: high production costs, technology availability, infrastructure development, competition, and international market access.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent judgment on Centre-state fiscal powers emphasizes cooperative federalism. Analyze the constitutional provisions governing Centre-state financial relations and critically evaluate how judicial interpretations contribute to strengthening or challenging the federal structure in India. Suggest reforms to enhance fiscal autonomy of states.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States; Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure; Devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by explaining the recent SC judgment and the concept of cooperative federalism. Detail constitutional provisions (e.g., Articles 246, 268, 270, 280, GST Council). Discuss how judicial pronouncements shape the federal balance. Analyze challenges to fiscal autonomy. Propose reforms like greater state control over taxation, clearer revenue sharing formulas, and enhanced role of the Finance Commission.

mainsMocks.question: India's growing engagement in Arctic governance reflects its expanding strategic and scientific interests. Examine the geopolitical significance of the Arctic region and discuss how India's updated Arctic Policy contributes to its climate diplomacy and national interests. What are the potential opportunities and environmental concerns for India in the Arctic?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighborhood- relations; Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate. GS3: Environmental pollution and degradation, conservation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's enhanced Arctic engagement and policy. Explain Arctic's geopolitical significance (resource potential, trade routes, climate change impact). Detail how India's policy aligns with climate diplomacy (research on monsoon, environmental protection) and national interests (scientific collaboration, resource exploration). Discuss opportunities (scientific knowledge, resources, shipping routes) and environmental concerns (melting ice, biodiversity loss, sustainable exploitation).

quickBytes.text: June 23 is observed as United Nations Public Service Day, recognizing value of public service.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to achieve a production capacity of 5 million metric tonnes by 2030.
The 'Extended Producer Responsibility' (EPR) framework makes producers responsible for post-consumer waste management.
ISRO's Satish Dhawan Space Centre is located at Sriharikota, Andhra Pradesh.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises 6 members, 3 from RBI and 3 appointed by the government.
Article 44 of the Indian Constitution is a Directive Principle of State Policy related to the Uniform Civil Code.
India is an observer state of the Arctic Council, an intergovernmental forum promoting cooperation in the Arctic.
The 'Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan' (Self-Reliant India Campaign) was announced in 2020.
The Indus Valley Civilization is also known as the Harappan Civilization.
The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body established under Article 324.

GS1.explainer:
• A team of international researchers has published new findings proposing novel computational and linguistic approaches to decipher the enigmatic Indus Valley Script.
• The research utilizes advanced AI and pattern recognition techniques to analyze the approximately 4,000 surviving inscriptions from the Harappan civilization.
• While a definitive decipherment remains elusive, the study suggests potential linguistic structures and phonetic patterns within the script, hinting at its underlying language family.
• The Indus Valley Script, one of the world's oldest writing systems, has baffled archaeologists and linguists for decades due to the absence of bilingual inscriptions.
• Successful decipherment could unlock unprecedented insights into the social, economic, and religious life of the ancient Indus Valley Civilization.
